text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. One useful method is to set SMART goals. This means your goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying “finish the report,” say “complete the final draft by Friday.” It’s also important to break your project into smaller milestones. Each milestone marks an important step, like finishing the research or getting feedback. At each milestone, think about what you will deliver, such as a presentation or a written summary. Finally, make sure all stakeholders agree on these goals. Alignment helps avoid confusion later and keeps everyone working in the same direction.
